Combinatorics & Math Patterns

Overview

Google interviews frequently test math/combinatorics reasoning — more than other FAANGs. This covers number theory, counting, geometry, and probability patterns common in coding interviews.

Key Properties

  • When to Use: Problem involves counting arrangements, modular arithmetic, GCD/LCM, prime numbers, or geometric calculations
  • Google Signal: Can you derive a formula instead of brute-forcing?

Pattern 1: Modular Arithmetic

Basics

1(a + b) % m = ((a % m) + (b % m)) % m
2(a * b) % m = ((a % m) * (b % m)) % m
3(a - b) % m = ((a % m) - (b % m) + m) % m   ← add m to avoid negative
4
5Division: (a / b) % m = (a * b^(-1)) % m  where b^(-1) = modular inverse

Modular Exponentiation (Fast Power)

1// Time: O(log exp), Space: O(1)
2long modPow(long base, long exp, long mod) {
3    long result = 1;
4    base %= mod;
5    while (exp > 0) {
6        if ((exp & 1) == 1) result = result * base % mod;
7        exp >>= 1;
8        base = base * base % mod;
9    }
10    return result;
11}

1# Python has built-in: pow(base, exp, mod)

Modular Inverse (when mod is prime)

1b^(-1) mod p = b^(p-2) mod p    (Fermat's little theorem)

Classic LC: LC 1808 (Maximize Number of Nice Divisors), LC 372 (Super Pow)

Pattern 2: GCD / LCM

1int gcd(int a, int b) { return b == 0 ? a : gcd(b, a % b); }
2long lcm(long a, long b) { return a / gcd(a, b) * b; }  // divide first to avoid overflow

1from math import gcd
2lcm = a * b // gcd(a, b)
3# Python 3.9+: math.lcm(a, b)

Classic LC:

  • LC 1071 GCD of Strings — O(N+M)
  • LC 878 Nth Magical Number — LCM + binary search
  • LC 2344 Minimum Deletions to Make Array Divisible — GCD of target array

Pattern 3: Prime Numbers & Sieve

Sieve of Eratosthenes

1// Time: O(N log log N), Space: O(N)
2boolean[] sieve(int n) {
3    boolean[] isPrime = new boolean[n + 1];
4    Arrays.fill(isPrime, true);
5    isPrime[0] = isPrime[1] = false;
6    for (int i = 2; i * i <= n; i++) {
7        if (isPrime[i]) {
8            for (int j = i * i; j <= n; j += i) {
9                isPrime[j] = false;
10            }
11        }
12    }
13    return isPrime;
14}

Prime Factorization

1// Time: O(√N)
2List<int[]> primeFactors(int n) {
3    List<int[]> factors = new ArrayList<>();
4    for (int i = 2; i * i <= n; i++) {
5        int count = 0;
6        while (n % i == 0) { n /= i; count++; }
7        if (count > 0) factors.add(new int[]{i, count});
8    }
9    if (n > 1) factors.add(new int[]{n, 1});
10    return factors;
11}

Classic LC: LC 204 (Count Primes), LC 952 (Largest Component by Common Factor)

Pattern 4: Combinations & Counting

nCr with Pascal’s Triangle

1// Time: O(N·K), Space: O(N·K)
2long[][] pascal(int n) {
3    long[][] C = new long[n + 1][n + 1];
4    for (int i = 0; i <= n; i++) {
5        C[i][0] = 1;
6        for (int j = 1; j <= i; j++) {
7            C[i][j] = C[i-1][j-1] + C[i-1][j];  // add % MOD if needed
8        }
9    }
10    return C;
11}

nCr with Modular Inverse (for large N)

1MOD = 10**9 + 7
2
3def nCr(n, r):
4    if r > n: return 0
5    num = den = 1
6    for i in range(r):
7        num = num * (n - i) % MOD
8        den = den * (i + 1) % MOD
9    return num * pow(den, MOD - 2, MOD) % MOD

Catalan Numbers

1C(n) = C(2n, n) / (n+1) = (2n)! / ((n+1)! * n!)
2
3C(0)=1, C(1)=1, C(2)=2, C(3)=5, C(4)=14, C(5)=42
4
5Applications:
6  - Number of valid parentheses sequences of length 2n
7  - Number of unique BSTs with n nodes
8  - Number of ways to triangulate a polygon with n+2 sides

Classic LC: LC 96 (Unique BSTs), LC 22 (Generate Parentheses count)

Pattern 5: Reservoir Sampling & Random

Reservoir Sampling (K=1)

1// Select 1 random element from stream of unknown length
2// Each element has 1/n probability of being chosen
3// Time: O(N), Space: O(1)
4Random rand = new Random();
5int result = 0;
6int count = 0;
7for (int val : stream) {
8    count++;
9    if (rand.nextInt(count) == 0) {
10        result = val;
11    }
12}

Classic LC: LC 382 (Linked List Random Node), LC 398 (Random Pick Index)

Fisher-Yates Shuffle

1// Time: O(N), Space: O(1) extra
2void shuffle(int[] arr) {
3    Random rand = new Random();
4    for (int i = arr.length - 1; i > 0; i--) {
5        int j = rand.nextInt(i + 1);
6        int tmp = arr[i]; arr[i] = arr[j]; arr[j] = tmp;
7    }
8}

Classic LC: LC 384 (Shuffle an Array)

Weighted Random / Binary Search on Prefix Sum

Classic LC: LC 528 (Random Pick with Weight) — O(log N) per pick

Pattern 6: Geometry / Computational Geometry

Cross Product (Orientation Test)

1// Returns > 0 if counter-clockwise, < 0 if clockwise, 0 if collinear
2long cross(int[] O, int[] A, int[] B) {
3    return (long)(A[0] - O[0]) * (B[1] - O[1])
4         - (long)(A[1] - O[1]) * (B[0] - O[0]);
5}

Convex Hull (Andrew’s Monotone Chain)

1# Time: O(N log N), Space: O(N)
2def convex_hull(points):
3    points.sort()
4    lower = []
5    for p in points:
6        while len(lower) >= 2 and cross(lower[-2], lower[-1], p) <= 0:
7            lower.pop()
8        lower.append(p)
9    upper = []
10    for p in reversed(points):
11        while len(upper) >= 2 and cross(upper[-2], upper[-1], p) <= 0:
12            upper.pop()
13        upper.append(p)
14    return lower[:-1] + upper[:-1]
15
16def cross(O, A, B):
17    return (A[0]-O[0])*(B[1]-O[1]) - (A[1]-O[1])*(B[0]-O[0])

Distance & Line Formulas

1Euclidean distance: sqrt((x2-x1)² + (y2-y1)²)
2Manhattan distance: |x2-x1| + |y2-y1|
3
4Tip: Avoid sqrt when possible — compare squared distances instead.
5Tip: Use long to avoid integer overflow in distance calculations.

Classic LC:

  • LC 149 Max Points on a Line — GCD for slope representation, O(N²)
  • LC 587 Erect the Fence — Convex Hull
  • LC 973 K Closest Points to Origin — O(N) quickselect or O(N log K) heap
  • LC 1232 Check if Straight Line — Cross product

Pattern 7: Bit Counting & Number Theory

Count Divisors

1Number of divisors of n = product of (e_i + 1) for each prime factor p_i^e_i
2Example: 12 = 2² × 3¹ → (2+1)(1+1) = 6 divisors

Euler’s Totient

1φ(n) = count of integers in [1, n] coprime to n
2φ(p) = p - 1  for prime p
3φ(p^k) = p^k - p^(k-1)

Sum of Digits / Digital Root

1Digital root of n = 1 + (n-1) % 9  (for n > 0)

Classic LC: LC 258 (Add Digits), LC 1922 (Count Good Numbers)

LC Example

# Problem Pattern Time Space
204 Count Primes Sieve O(N log log N) O(N)
96 Unique BSTs Catalan O(N) O(N)
382 Linked List Random Node Reservoir sampling O(N) O(1)
528 Random Pick with Weight Prefix sum + BS O(N) build, O(log N) pick O(N)
149 Max Points on a Line GCD slope O(N²) O(N)
587 Erect the Fence Convex Hull O(N log N) O(N)
878 Nth Magical Number LCM + binary search O(log(N·max(A,B))) O(1)
952 Largest Component Sieve + Union-Find O(N√M) O(M)
1071 GCD of Strings GCD O(N+M) O(1)
384 Shuffle an Array Fisher-Yates O(N) O(1)
372 Super Pow Mod exponentiation O(N) O(1)
1808 Max Nice Divisors Mod exp + math O(log N) O(1)
